meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your app fails. Learn how to implement analytics to debug code effectively as a student using Antigravity and Claude Code tools.
content.answer_block: To solve unidentifiable bugs, stop guessing and start measuring. By integrating event-based analytics, you turn invisible runtime failures into actionable data points. Use Antigravity to instrument your core logic and Claude Code to automate the deployment of these telemetry probes, providing immediate visibility into where and why your code breaks.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a cryptic error message, and your heart sinks. You have spent hours staring at the logic, convinced the code is flawless, yet it refuses to run. This is the 'Black Box' phase of every developer's journey—where you are coding in the dark, unable to see the silent failures happening behind the UI.

But what if you could turn on the lights? By shifting from manual guessing to data-driven observability, you stop being a programmer who prays and start being an engineer who knows. This case study shows you how to instrument your first project, transforming your debugging process from a frustrating chore into a surgical strike.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Path
steps.description: Before adding code, identify the three most important user actions. Analytics is useless without focus; track what defines a 'successful' session.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest the three most critical user interaction points that, if they fail, would break the core value proposition of the app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'List the core functions in this project that handle user state and data persistence.'

steps.title: Instrument the Entry Points
steps.description: Inject logging at the start of your main functions. This captures the input state before the logic executes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a wrapper function template that logs function execution, input arguments, and current timestamp to a persistent JSON file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the logging wrapper to all functions in src/logic.js'

steps.title: Implement Error Boundary Tracking
steps.description: Ensure that when an error occurs, the context (state, input, stack) is captured before the app crashes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a centralized error handler that captures the global state and logs the full stack trace to an analytics endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the global error handler to integrate the new logging utility.'

steps.title: Configure Data Visualization
steps.description: Use your analytics tool to create a dashboard that displays the frequency of errors versus successful events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a configuration schema for an analytics dashboard that tracks the ratio of successful function calls to error logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a script that parses my local log files and outputs a summary of error counts per function.'

steps.title: Automate Telemetry Audits
steps.description: Use Claude Code to periodically check if your new features are being instrumented automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that verifies if new functions added to the codebase are correctly wrapped with the telemetry logging utility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a pre-commit hook that warns if a new function is missing the logging wrapper.'

pro_tips: Always log the 'before' and 'after' state of variables, not just the function name.
Use a unique 'Session ID' for every user interaction to reconstruct the timeline of a bug.
Silence your logs in production but keep them verbose in staging to avoid data noise.
Focus on tracking 'State Transitions' rather than individual lines of code.
Treat your analytics data as a product feature; it is the most valuable asset in your portfolio.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, force a bug in your staging environment to verify that your dashboard captures the event accurately.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student guessing at syntax; you are an engineer building with visibility. Take control of your development lifecycle and stop wasting time on invisible bugs. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Inject logging at the start of your main functions. This captures the input state before the logic executes.
Ensure that when an error occurs, the context (state, input, stack) is captured before the app crashes.
Use your analytics tool to create a dashboard that displays the frequency of errors versus successful events.
